title: Machine Learning Strategy E2E Framework Foundation
goal_statement: Start the local/static/fixture ML Strategy E2E framework foundation after CR151 statistical admission capability.
scope_summary: CR152 covers CP0/CP1/CP2 first, then ML strategy foundation contracts after approval.
First wave covers PIT feature matrix, label policy/leakage guard, purged + embargo CV, training snapshot/model artifact metadata, prediction artifact and ML admission gate.

CR152 remains static/fixture-only and does not authorize real lake, NAS, provider, QMT, runtime, simulation/live/trading, broker, credential, external framework, Git remote or catalog pointer operations.
approval_focus: CP5 must review S01-S04 full LLD and S05 technical note before implementation; triple_barrier first-wave active enforcement should be BLOCKED; adapter/contract mapping deepening may require meta-se dispatch or explicit inline-fallback.
approve_effect: CP5 approval allows local/static/fixture source implementation and tests only; it does not authorize real training, real data validation, registry/store/catalog writes, runtime or external operations.
reject_effect: CR152 remains cp2_pending, is narrowed, or is cancelled by user decision.
not_authorized_by_approve: feature store / label store / model registry / prediction store writes
real model training or real data validation
decision_burden: CP6 implementation passed; CR152 is ready for local/static/fixture CP7 verification.
remaining_risks: Fixture-only validation must not be represented as real model performance or production readiness.
Model artifact metadata must not be implemented as model registry write/publish/promote/upload/set_current or catalog pointer mutation.
CR152 ML contracts must extend or compose existing ResearchDatasetSpec / TrainingSnapshotSpec / ModelArtifactRef anchors instead of creating parallel contract families.
STATE v2 hygiene remains an accepted process caveat from CR151 and must not be expanded into CR152 scope.
split_rationale: CR152 follows CR151 because ML admission should reuse shared admission status semantics while keeping ML-specific validation methods separate from multifactor statistical gate internals.
why_not_merge_with_parent: The roadmap is a planning artifact; CR152 needs a formal CP2 scope baseline before design and implementation.
why_not_story_or_task: The change affects ML strategy framework scope, admission semantics, testing, evidence and release gates, so it requires formal CR and checkpoint flow.
user_goal_impact: Improves ML strategy framework reliability before real training, runtime, paper/live or data-lake production work is considered.
cp3_attention_items: CP3 must separate model artifact metadata contract from model registry write/publish behavior; model registry writes remain not authorized.
CP3 must extend existing TrainingSnapshotSpec / ModelArtifactRef related contracts instead of creating parallel ML contracts.
CP3 must create CP3 discussion log/checkpoint if Architecture Gray Areas are explored; CP2 discussion log N/A remains accepted.
cp5_attention_items: S01 CP5 LLD must define active triple_barrier first-wave enforcement exactly; recommended BLOCKED because algorithm is not implemented.
S04 CP5 LLD must record meta-se dispatch or explicit inline-fallback if adapter / contract delta field mapping needs architecture deepening.
CP5 approval is required before source implementation; real training, real data validation, registry/store/catalog writes and runtime remain not authorized.
cp6_attention_items: S03: treat engine/research_production_contracts.py as read-only compatibility inspection by default; prefer engine/training_snapshot_contract.py / engine/research_manifest.py for metadata companions, and stop for CP5/merge-owner confirmation before modifying the shared contract file.
S04: inspect StrategyAdmissionPackage source before changing engine/strategy_admission_package.py; if safe gate linkage extension is not obvious, stop and reopen design or dispatch meta-se.
S05: factor-research-loop TEST-PLAN/TASKS refs are CP7/CP8 wording-alignment references only; CR152 fixture-only/no-real-operation boundary wins on conflict.
